name: pressure_fed_lox_kerosene_system_8e3b56bd
description: Design a pressure-fed rocket feed system using LOX (liquid oxygen) and kerosene as propellants, with nitrogen (GN2) as the pressurant gas. The system must deliver both propellants to a rocket engine block under pressure-fed conditions. FIXED quantities: component architecture (GN2 tank, LOX tank, kerosene tank, engine, pressurization lines, feed lines), propellant types (LOX, kerosene, GN2). TUNABLE quantities: tank pressures, tank volumes, line sizes, pressurization rates, flow rates. The design must ensure both propellants flow to the engine and the system operates without nonphysical warnings.

notes: Use pressure_fed_lox_kerosene seed for baseline architecture
Ensure GN2 pressurizes both LOX and kerosene tanks
Both propellants must flow to engine
The downstream node named engine is an Ambient demand boundary for feed-flow checks.
Tune feed CdA values before changing topology.
If physical warnings mention a component, fix that component first.

checks.description: Simulation must complete without crashing or invalid configuration
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
LOX must flow through lox_feed_line to engine
Kerosene must flow through kerosene_feed_line to engine
GN2 must flow to pressurize LOX tank
GN2 must flow to pressurize kerosene tank
LOX feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
Kerosene feed line delivers non-zero mass flow to the engine.
